section.name: Issue tracker
section.p: BreakTest uses GitHub Issues for BreakTest-specific bugs, regressions, feature requests, and documentation feedback.
section.note: BreakTest is independent from The Apache Software Foundation. If you are reporting a behavior difference from Apache JMeter, please include the Apache JMeter version you compared against.

section.name: Support questions
section.p: GitHub Issues are best for actionable BreakTest bugs and feature requests. For usage questions, include a minimal JMX file, the exact BreakTest version, Java version, operating system, and what you already tried. If your question is about generic JMeter-compatible test-plan modeling, upstream Apache JMeter community resources may still be useful background, but BreakTest issues should stay focused on BreakTest behavior.

section.name: Requesting an enhancement
section.p: Please check whether the same enhancement has already been requested. If a similar issue exists, add detail to the existing discussion instead of opening a duplicate.

Good feature requests describe the use case first. For BreakTest, it is especially helpful to say whether the request affects compatibility with existing JMX files, resource usage, HTTP/2 behavior, controller semantics, GUI editing, AI-assisted scripting, or BreakTest Enterprise handoff.
If you already have a concrete implementation, open a pull request from a fork. Small documentation fixes do not need a separate issue.

section.name: Raising an issue
section.p: First check the BreakTest issue tracker. If the issue appears to be inherited from Apache JMeter, please say so and include the upstream version you tested. If the behavior is specific to a BreakTest feature, include enough detail for maintainers to reproduce it.

section.name: Required information for bug reporting
section.p: Please provide enough information for others to reproduce the report:

ul.li: Java version, from java -version; BreakTest requires Java 21 or later
jmeter.log or other relevant log files, after checking for secrets
Minimal JMX file if relevant, with secrets and private endpoints redacted
JTL/result files if relevant, with sensitive information redacted
Whether third-party plugins are required to reproduce the behavior
Whether the same plan behaves differently in Apache JMeter 5.6.3
Steps to reproduce, expected behavior, and actual behavior
Screenshots or screen recordings for GUI issues

section.p: New source files should use the existing Apache License header style used in this repository. Do not add @author tags; credit is handled through Git history and release notes.
